title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum is set to play against the Washington Wizards after missing a game for injury management.
The Celtics star has a minutes restriction as he returns from a right ruptured Achilles suffered 10 months ago.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable to play; Nikola Vucevic remains out with a finger fracture.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine' starring Alan Ritchson is now streaming on Netflix, featuring a soldier confronting a lethal war machine.
'Zootopia 2,' featuring Jason Bateman and Ginnifer Goodwin, is available on Disney+ following its Oscar nomination.
Steve Carell stars in HBO's 'Rooster,' a comedy about a divorced author starting fresh on his daughter's college campus.

title: 5 things to know about Patriots offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots sign former Jets guard Alijah Vera-Tucker to bolster offensive line after high sack count last season.
Vera-Tucker, a versatile lineman, missed last season due to triceps injury; he has a history of injuries.
Morgan Moses, a former teammate, influenced Vera-Tucker's decision to join the Patriots for their winning culture.

title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora identified Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as the team's leaders for the 2026 season.
Trevor Story led the team in key offensive categories in 2025 and is known for his lead-by-example approach.
Garrett Crochet, Red Sox's ace, excelled in 2025 with 255 strikeouts, drawing comparisons to top pitchers like Randy Johnson.

title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC will make its NWSL debut against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium on March 14.
Head coach Filipa Patão plans a possession-style game, with attack-focused strategies potentially exposing the defense.
The match will be played on the new grass field at Gillette, possibly aiding Legacy FC's style of play.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers consider legislation to stop changing clocks twice a year, supported by Senate President Karen Spilka.
The proposed bill, filed by State Senator John F. Keenan, aims for Massachusetts to observe Atlantic Standard Time permanently.
Research links time changes to health issues; the proposal is under Senate review amid concerns about daylight hours.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People announces the 2026 Pride parade and festival will take place on June 6.
The parade will start at 11 a.m. in Copley Square and end with a festival at Boston Common.
Mayor Michelle Wu praised the event's inclusivity, and volunteers can sign up through the organization's website.

title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 14-year-old Brazilian girl detained by ICE in Marlborough was released and returned to Massachusetts by federal officials.
The girl, living with her U.S. citizen brothers, was detained while with two alleged gang members in Marlborough.
A federal judge ordered her release; she must appear in immigration court as the government may attempt to deport her.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins currently hold the final wild-card spot in the Eastern Conference by one point.
David Pastrnak and Elias Lindholm struggle in 5-on-5 play, indicating a need for lineup changes.
Bruins' power play performance has dropped to 14.3% with four goals scored in the last eight games.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Swiss researcher Alexis Cogne is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support trauma survivors through the Stepping Strong Center.
Cogne, 26, is a visiting researcher at Harvard Medical School specializing in neurorehabilitation, focusing on helping patients regain independence after injuries.
He aims to raise $10,000 for research and rehabilitation programs at Brigham and Women's Hospital, highlighting the importance of funding in this area.
